name: Universal Office Beta Release Roadmap
purpose: Canonical machine-readable roadmap for Beta Release planning, phase sequencing, governance questions, tranche sizing, and Admin Dashboard visualization.

current_repo_state.structure_json_role: Project manifest, implementation ledger, governance history, legacy admin todo source, and current state pointer.
current_repo_state.roadmap_role: Canonical release architecture and phase/tranche planning source for Admin Dashboard roadmap visualization.

release_identity.release_model: First release represents each major apostolic tradition family through one governed initial recension or tradition lane, then expands by tradition, jurisdiction, calendar, and depth over time.
non_negotiables: Beta Release is not Byzantine-centered in public presentation.
BCP / Anglican Daily Office is the public-facing focus.
Roman Catholic Divine Office / Liturgy of the Hours representation is a Beta Release blocker.
Russian / Slavic Byzantine Horologion is the first Eastern Orthodox recension.
Ethiopian / Tewahedo Sa'atat is the first Oriental Orthodox lane.
East Syriac Hudra is the first Church of the East lane.
Other traditions, jurisdictions, and profiles are later expansions, not first-release requirements.
Mobile onboarding must not present the traditions as a smorgasbord of liturgical options.
Anglicanism is the default grammar and fallback, but the first-use experience must not be Anglican-anxious or denominationally defensive.
Eastern Christian routing must not make Byzantine Christianity normative; Church of the East, Oriental Orthodox, and Eastern Orthodox should be listed in chronological order of differentiation.
Tradition-expansion notification is pastoral service, not marketing.
release_identity.mobile_product_thesis: A design-forward digital breviary that opens like a chapel: Anglican in grammar, catholic and orthodox in inheritance, beautiful because the prayer itself is beautiful.
release_identity.accepted_welcome_copy: The Church's daily prayer, appointed for the hour. Begin where you pray, and let the office lead you.
release_identity.anglican_default_posture: Anglican-first by default grammar and fallback, not Anglican-forward as a self-conscious public posture. The app receives the user, routes them to their tradition, and explains the Anglican center only when needed.

release_doctrine.beta_blocker_definition: A defect, absence, or governance gap that prevents honest first public release under the required major-family representation model.
beta_blockers_include: A required major apostolic family has no honest first-release representation.
A public-facing beta path crashes or fails to render.
A baseline office is advertised as present but lacks enough structure or text to be liturgically honest.
The Admin Dashboard misrepresents project state or release blockers.
Ordinary user flow exposes development placeholders, undefined values, stale implementation language, or hidden silent fallbacks.
Roman Catholic Divine Office / Liturgy of the Hours baseline is absent.
not_beta_blockers_if_honestly_deferred: Greek, Antiochian, or other non-Slavic Eastern Orthodox profiles.
Coptic Agpeya as a separate Oriental Orthodox lane, provided Ethiopian is stable as first OO representation.
Armenian, Syriac Orthodox, and other Oriental Orthodox additions beyond the Ethiopian first lane.
1960 Roman Breviary, Benedictine, monastic, or other Latin variants if the first Roman Catholic Divine Office baseline is present.
Full database/typed-element Phase 5 migration, provided release data remains honest and stable.
honesty_rules: Do not hide true corpus gaps as UX polish.
Do not present one jurisdictional recension as the entire tradition family.
Do not conflate Ethiopian Sa'atat with Coptic Agpeya.
Do not treat East Syriac Hudra as missing.
Do not call the Universal Office release-ready while Roman Catholic Divine Office representation is absent.

Do not claim Phase 5 typed architecture is complete until it is pressure-tested against Anglican, Roman Catholic, Byzantine, East Syriac, Ethiopian, and any distinct Coptic Agpeya lane pursued before that milestone.

mobile_onboarding_doctrine.welcome_copy: The Church's daily prayer, appointed for the hour. Begin where you pray, and let the office lead you.

current_routes.Latin Catholic: Liturgy of the Hours / Roman office baseline, subject to source-governance and public rendering authorization

eastern_branch.order_note: Listed in chronological order of differentiation so Byzantine/Eastern Orthodox does not become the implicit norm for all Eastern Christianity.

unsure_branch.copy_rule: Explain the Anglican default only after the user selects I'm not sure; do not surface this as a standing note on the main routing screen.

mobile_onboarding_doctrine.branching_rule: Once the user is in a primary tradition, offer further branching from there only when multiple governed forms exist.

mobile_visual_design_doctrine.core_metaphor: A digital breviary that opens like a chapel.
mobile_visual_design_doctrine.anti_slop_rule: Every visible design decision should arise from liturgical function, theological meaning, ecclesial beauty, or the office's own order, not from generic sacred vibes.
mobile_visual_design_doctrine.beauty_rule: Beauty is not ornament; it is prayer made visible. The app should evoke incense, monastery, cathedral, and Easter Vigil beauty while avoiding cartoon high-church overload and AI sacred luxury.
mobile_visual_design_doctrine.tradition_register_rule: Tradition-specific aesthetics may subtly modify atmosphere, accent, typography, ornamental grammar, and terminology when rooted in that tradition's own prayer, book culture, calendar, or ecclesial memory. They must not become costume skins.

coverage_notice_doctrine.rule: When a selected tradition-family has only partial current coverage, show a calm threshold information screen before routing to the available governed form.
coverage_notice_doctrine.oriental_orthodox_notice: This release currently begins with the Ethiopian tradition. The liturgies of the other Oriental Orthodox churches, including Coptic, Armenian, West Syriac / Malankara, and Eritrean traditions, will be included in future updates of The Universal Office.
coverage_notice_doctrine.eastern_orthodox_notice: This release currently begins with the Byzantine Horologion, with English-language coverage slanted toward Slavic usage. Future updates will broaden jurisdictional and local-use support, including Greek, Antiochian, OCA, ROCOR, and other received Orthodox usages where source-governed material is available.

notification_signup.primary_label: Notify me when this tradition expands
notification_signup.rule: The signup must be opt-in, visually secondary, scoped to the selected tradition-family, and framed as service rather than marketing.

major_tradition_families.blocker_reason: The BCP path is the public-facing beta center and must pass public-flow hardening.

major_tradition_families.first_release_lane: Divine Office / Liturgy of the Hours
major_tradition_families.blocker_reason: Roman Catholic Divine Office / Liturgy of the Hours baseline is required before Beta Release.
major_tradition_families.corpus_repo_note: Restricted private corpus/governance repository for Roman Liturgy of the Hours. Contains source governance, permission registry, office structure, calendar scaffold, office-slot resolver map, calendar-office context map, source-unit target map, and a verified synthetic resolver. Private restricted corpus build-out may proceed in JWJeffery/LOTH while permission is pending. Official LOTH text must not be exposed publicly, fetched by PrayerAppNew, or rendered externally until permission authorizes it.
major_tradition_families.render_disabled_note: Roman LOTH public rendering in PrayerAppNew remains disabled until a permission record in JWJeffery/LOTH explicitly authorizes external display. Private restricted corpus development may continue in JWJeffery/LOTH while permission is pending. PrayerAppNew must not fetch from the private repo or expose official LOTH text publicly before authorization.
major_tradition_families.recommended_first_profile: Current Roman Liturgy of the Hours / Divine Office ordinary form

synthetic_resolver.notes: All nine Roman LOTH office IDs resolve synthetically. Daytime aliases bind to the Daytime Prayer template. This proves architecture flow only; it does not authorize or enable public rendering. It also does not prohibit private restricted corpus build-out in JWJeffery/LOTH while permission is pending.

later_expansions: Old Calendar / New Calendar profile refinement
Broader Menaion and Typikon collision handling
major_tradition_families.mobile_coverage_notice: This release currently begins with the Byzantine Horologion, with English-language coverage slanted toward Slavic usage. Future updates will broaden jurisdictional and local-use support, including Greek, Antiochian, OCA, ROCOR, and other received Orthodox usages where source-governed material is available.

major_tradition_families.blocker_reason: Ethiopian lane must be audited and stabilized enough to serve as honest first Oriental Orthodox representation.
do_not_conflate: Ethiopian Sa'atat is not Coptic Agpeya.
Coptic Agpeya is a separate later Oriental Orthodox lane unless explicitly pulled into Beta Release by governance decision.
later_expansions: Additional Oriental Orthodox calendars and synaxaria
major_tradition_families.mobile_coverage_notice: This release currently begins with the Ethiopian tradition. The liturgies of the other Oriental Orthodox churches, including Coptic, Armenian, West Syriac / Malankara, and Eritrean traditions, will be included in future updates of The Universal Office.

major_tradition_families.blocker_reason: Hudra lane must be audited and stabilized enough to serve as honest first Church of the East representation.
major_tradition_families.mobile_routing_order_note: Listed before Oriental Orthodox and Eastern Orthodox in the Eastern Christian branch to avoid Byzantine normativity and preserve chronological differentiation.

phase_plan.title: Roadmap extraction and mission reset
phase_plan.purpose: Create the roadmap source of truth and stop coding against stale Byzantine-centered release assumptions.
phase_plan.exit_gate: project_roadmap.json exists, validates, and accurately encodes BCP-facing beta identity, major-family representation requirements, Roman Catholic release blocker status, and phase/tranche doctrine.

phase_plan.title: Admin Dashboard roadmap visualization
phase_plan.purpose: Make the Admin Dashboard visualize the whole Universal Office beta roadmap instead of only the Byzantine release lane.
phase_plan.exit_gate: Admin Dashboard displays release identity, major-family coverage matrix, blockers, phase/tranche board, and governance-question queue from project_roadmap.json.

phase_plan.title: BCP public-facing hardening
phase_plan.purpose: Harden the Anglican / BCP Daily Office path because it is the public-facing center of Beta Release.
phase_plan.exit_gate: BCP Daily Office, Book of Needs, mode switching, BCP-only behavior, and public UI flow pass browser QC and human review.

phase_plan.title: Roman Catholic Divine Office baseline
phase_plan.purpose: Add required Roman Catholic Divine Office / Liturgy of the Hours first-release representation.
phase_plan.exit_gate: Roman Catholic Divine Office lane exists, renders an honest ordinary baseline, and no longer blocks Beta Release.
phase_plan.governance_note: External private corpus repository JWJeffery/LOTH scaffolded 2026-05-23. Permission inquiry sent to DivineOffice.org / Surgeworks 2026-05-23; status pending. As of 2026-05-23, LOTH/main validates 26 JSON files and npm run verify:synthetic passes: all nine Roman LOTH office IDs resolve through the synthetic resolver, including Daytime Prayer aliases, with emitsOfficialText=false and renderPolicy=blocked_until_authorized. Phase 3 may continue building the private restricted LOTH corpus and internal resolver architecture while permission is pending. Public app rendering, external display, and PrayerAppNew-side private-repo fetch remain blocked until permission authorization is recorded.

phase_plan.title: Eastern Orthodox Russian / Slavic release closeout
phase_plan.purpose: Confirm the existing Slavic Byzantine Horologion lane is stable and honestly scoped for Beta Release.
phase_plan.exit_gate: Russian / Slavic EO lane passes release-visible QC and dashboard correctly classifies deferred Byzantine depth as non-blocking.

phase_plan.title: Oriental Orthodox Ethiopian release closeout
phase_plan.purpose: Confirm Ethiopian Sa'atat is stable and honestly scoped as first Oriental Orthodox representation.
phase_plan.exit_gate: Ethiopian lane passes release-visible QC and dashboard correctly distinguishes Ethiopian Sa'atat from future Coptic Agpeya.

phase_plan.title: Church of the East Hudra release closeout
phase_plan.purpose: Confirm East Syriac Hudra is stable and honestly scoped as first Church of the East representation.
phase_plan.exit_gate: Hudra lane passes release-visible QC and dashboard treats it as present but refinement-pending, not missing.

phase_plan.title: Cross-family shell and release-hardening pass
phase_plan.purpose: Verify the app behaves as one coherent product across all first-release lanes.
phase_plan.exit_gate: All public modes, sidebars, dashboard views, deferred-language handling, mobile layout, and ordinary user flows pass final hardening checks.

phase_plan.title: Final Beta gate
phase_plan.purpose: Run final Beta Release QC, close or explicitly defer every known item, and prepare release ledger/tag.
phase_plan.exit_gate: No beta blockers remain; deferred depth ledger is explicit; README, dashboard, and release identity agree.

tranche_plan.title: Seed project_roadmap.json
tranche_plan.purpose: Create canonical roadmap source of truth.
qc: Confirm release identity and blocker doctrine

tranche_plan.title: Point repo governance toward roadmap source
tranche_plan.purpose: Correct stale release-focus language and record project_roadmap.json as roadmap source without migrating dashboard logic yet.

tranche_plan.title: Add Admin roadmap loader and summary panel
tranche_plan.purpose: Fetch project_roadmap.json and render Universal Beta Roadmap summary without removing existing Byzantine cockpit.

tranche_plan.title: Render family coverage matrix and phase board
tranche_plan.purpose: Visualize major tradition-family coverage and phase/tranche progress.
qc: No hard-coded roadmap content beyond labels

tranche_plan.title: Render governance-question board
tranche_plan.purpose: Expose open source, release, architecture, UI, and future-expansion governance questions.
qc: Deferred decisions are visually distinct from blockers

tranche_plan.title: BCP public-flow QC
tranche_plan.purpose: Define and run browser QC for BCP public-facing path.

tranche_plan.title: Fix BCP beta-visible defects
tranche_plan.purpose: Repair defects discovered in BCP public-flow QC.

tranche_plan.title: Book of Needs public-flow QC and polish
tranche_plan.purpose: Ensure Book of Needs selector, rendering, and back-navigation are beta-ready.

tranche_plan.title: Roman Catholic Divine Office source-governance ruling
tranche_plan.purpose: Source-governance and private-corpus architecture scaffold established for the current Roman Liturgy of the Hours / Divine Office ordinary form. Permission remains pending; public rendering remains blocked. Private restricted corpus build-out may continue in JWJeffery/LOTH while awaiting permission.
qc: Private LOTH repo created and governed
Permission inquiry sent to DivineOffice.org / Surgeworks
PrayerAppNew does not fetch from the private repo
Official LOTH text is not exposed publicly
Private restricted corpus build-out may continue in JWJeffery/LOTH while permission is pending
Synthetic resolver verified in JWJeffery/LOTH with npm run verify:synthetic

tranche_plan.title: Roman LOTH composition-map and source-navigation governance
tranche_plan.purpose: Wire the accepted composition-map/source-navigation governance decision into the canonical project control files. Records: composition map accepted as core liturgical-assembly disclosure model; ribbon placement accepted as source-navigation metadata; current LOTH remains internal/restricted architecture-prototype track; future public-domain Roman-family corpus track follows internal LOTH stabilization; Roman office engine must remain source-edition-aware and not hardwired to current licensed LOTH text.
qc: No runtime JS, CSS, HTML, or corpus file modified

tranche_plan.title: Catholic office data schema scaffold
tranche_plan.purpose: Create first Roman Catholic office-family data contract after source-governance ruling.

tranche_plan.title: Catholic engine scaffold
tranche_plan.purpose: Add non-throwing Roman Catholic Divine Office resolver scaffold.
qc: No public UI route until shell tranche completes

tranche_plan.title: Catholic UI mode shell
tranche_plan.purpose: Add Roman Catholic Divine Office entry, sidebar shell, office selector, and basic renderer route.

tranche_plan.title: Catholic ordinary baseline corpus tranche 1
tranche_plan.purpose: Start with bounded Night Prayer / Compline baseline.

tranche_plan.title: Catholic Morning and Evening Prayer baseline
tranche_plan.purpose: Add honest ordinary baseline structure for Lauds and Vespers.
qc: Ordinary baseline is honest about deferred propers

tranche_plan.title: Catholic Office of Readings and Daytime Prayer baseline
tranche_plan.purpose: Add honest ordinary baseline structure for Office of Readings and Daytime Prayer.

tranche_plan.title: Catholic baseline release classification
tranche_plan.purpose: Mark Catholic representation from blocker to baseline-present only if QC supports it.

tranche_plan.title: Russian / Slavic Horologion release QC
tranche_plan.purpose: Run release-visible QC for the existing EO first-recension lane.

tranche_plan.title: Fix EO beta-visible defects only
tranche_plan.purpose: Repair only release-visible EO defects, not full seasonal/festal depth.

tranche_plan.title: Ethiopian release-lane audit
tranche_plan.purpose: Audit Ethiopian Sa'atat as first Oriental Orthodox release lane.
qc: Senkessar does not break public flow

tranche_plan.title: East Syriac Hudra release-lane audit
tranche_plan.purpose: Audit East Syriac Hudra as first Church of the East release lane.
qc: Saint silence remains governed where applicable

tranche_plan.title: Cross-family shell QC
tranche_plan.purpose: Test app as a single product across all public modes.

tranche_plan.title: Final Beta QC matrix
tranche_plan.purpose: Run final release matrix and prepare release ledger.
qc: Release tag only after clean pass

tranche_plan.title: Mobile product design and onboarding doctrine
tranche_plan.purpose: Record accepted mobile packaging doctrine: design-forward but not Anglican-forward; Anglican default without anxiety; received-tradition onboarding; East/West routing; chronological Eastern branch order; subtle tradition-specific visual registers; coverage honesty screens; and scoped tradition-expansion notification.
qc: No runtime JS, CSS, HTML, or corpus file modified

governance_questions.question: Should first-release Roman Catholic representation use the current Liturgy of the Hours / Divine Office as the first profile?
governance_questions.recommended_default: Yes. Use the current Roman Liturgy of the Hours / Divine Office ordinary form first; defer 1960 Breviary, Benedictine, and other Latin variants.
governance_questions.resolution: Use the current Roman Liturgy of the Hours / Divine Office ordinary form as the first Roman Catholic profile; defer 1960 Breviary, Benedictine, monastic, and other Latin variants.

governance_questions.question: What source/copyright strategy governs the Roman Catholic Divine Office baseline?
governance_questions.resolution: Private JWJeffery/LOTH corpus/governance repo holds source identification, permission records, architecture maps, and synthetic resolver. Private restricted corpus build-out may continue while permission is pending. Public rendering and external display remain blocked until permission is granted.

governance_questions.question: What is the minimum Roman Catholic Divine Office baseline that counts as honest Beta Release representation?
governance_questions.notes: Synthetic resolver proves office architecture, not a public baseline. Minimum baseline still requires permission-authorized text/rendering decision.

governance_questions.question: What defects, if any, remain in the BCP-facing public beta path?
What exact Ethiopian Sa'atat depth is required to represent Oriental Orthodoxy honestly in Beta Release?
What exact East Syriac Hudra depth is required to represent Church of the East honestly in Beta Release?
What source-governed policy should control Polyeleos / rank selection and Psalms 134-135 corpus?
How should annual fixed/movable Typikon collisions be handled across EO old/new calendar modes?
When are the major office families sufficiently represented to begin typed-element/database migration pressure-testing?

How should the Roman office engine disclose liturgical composition, handle printed-source navigation, and remain source-edition-agnostic across current restricted and future public-domain corpus tracks?
governance_questions.resolution: Composition map accepted as a core liturgical-assembly disclosure model. Ribbon placement accepted as source-navigation metadata and physical-book expression of liturgical composition. Current LOTH implementation remains an internal/restricted architecture-prototype track. After internal LOTH stabilization, a future public-domain Roman-family corpus track will reuse the Roman office engine and composition-map architecture. Roman office engine must remain source-edition-aware — distinguishing office-family architecture, source edition, calendar/rubrical regime, textual corpus, navigation affordances, source status, and public/restricted release status — and must not be hardwired to current licensed LOTH text.

dashboard_views.title: Release Identity
dashboard_views.purpose: Show BCP-facing beta identity and required major-family representation.

dashboard_views.title: Major Tradition-Family Coverage Matrix
dashboard_views.purpose: Show which required families are present, blocked, deferred, or ready.

dashboard_views.title: Beta Blockers
dashboard_views.purpose: Show blockers separately from honest deferred depth.

dashboard_views.title: Governance Questions
dashboard_views.purpose: Show open, decided, deferred, beta-blocking, and v1-blocking decisions.

micro_tranche.definition: One dangerous targeted repair, usually one file or one narrow bug.
use_when: Parser or syntax risk is high.
The change touches fragile engine logic.
A prior patch failed and must be corrected narrowly.

standard_tranche.definition: Default tranche size: 2-5 files, one coherent architectural purpose, complete QC.
use_when: One office-family feature or audit correction.

campaign_tranche.definition: 3-8 files allowed when changes are coherent, low-risk, and structurally repetitive.
use_when: Adding dashboard rendering from an already-defined schema.
Updating related documentation and roadmap state together.

forbidden_patterns: One tiny patch at a time when changes are safely batchable.
Massive unbounded patches that cannot be reviewed manually.
Full-file rewrites of structure.json or other large JSON files unless explicitly authorized.
Dashboard hard-coding that duplicates roadmap JSON content.

lucy.role: Architecture, roadmap governance, tranche sizing, Claude prompt design, patch review, QC design, and release classification.
must_do: Check whether Claude's patch size is safe.
Keep BCP-facing beta identity and major-family release model in view.
Classify appointed vs not-appointed vs deferred depth.
Identify what blocks beta versus what can be honestly deferred.

claude.role: Bounded patch generation after architecture and source-governance are settled.
must_do: Avoid full-file rewrites unless explicitly authorized.

phase_review_protocol.purpose: Prevent stale-roadmap drift and reassess project topology after each completed phase.
questions: Did this phase change the topology of the project?
Did we discover hidden release blockers?
Were any blockers reclassified as honest deferred depth?
Did any deferred item become beta-blocking?
Did Claude exceed safe patch complexity?
Did Marissa need narrower or better-sourced questions?
Did browser QC contradict static assumptions?
Should the next phase be split, merged, reordered, or paused?
Does the Admin Dashboard still visualize the true release state?
